boe_references.title: The Statement of Policy (SoP) shown on this page is effective from  01/07/2023. Past versions of this SoP can be found here.

paragraphs.summary: This statement of policy (SoP) sets out the calculation methodology that the Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA) uses, as the United Kingdom’s designated authority in respect of the Deposit Guarante
This methodology will apply only to the calculation of compensation cost levies.

This SoP is intended to be read together with the rules governing the funding of the FSCS in the Depositor Protection Part of the PRA Rulebook, and the sections relating to the role of the FSCS in fun
As described in Depositor Protection 42.3, the FSCS is required to calculate firm levies using the following formula (equation 1):

The risk-adjustment factor is composed of the member’s aggregate risk weight (ARW), multiplied by an adjustment factor (µ). The ARW for each member will be determined by the PRA (see Chapter 3) and pr
The PRA will calculate the ARW for each DGS member using the following formula (equation 2):

The ARW for each firm will range from 75% - 150%, which is the minimum range specified in the EBA guidelines. The PRA considers that this provides a sufficient degree of risk differentiation between f
For a DGS member that is a CRR firm,1 the calculation of the ARS will be based on the following risk indicators:

Each DGS member will be attributed an individual risk score (IRS) for each risk indicator (calibrated as in paragraph 3.4). The IRS for each risk indicator will then be weighted, as in the table above

Unless otherwise specified, all calculations are performed on each DGS member on a solo consolidated/unconsolidated basis, and terms used are as defined in the CRR.2 To calculate the values of the ris
The PRA will use the following calibration scales to attribute IRSs for each risk indicator for each DGS member that is a CRR firm in each levy cycle:
For a DGS member that is a credit union, the calculation of the ARS will be based on the following risk indicators:

Similar to the approach taken for CRR firms in paragraph 3.2, the PRA will calculate an IRS for each indicator for each DGS member that is a credit union, as calibrated in paragraph 3.8. These IRSs wi
To calculate the values of the risk indicators, the PRA will use:
The PRA will use the following calibration scales to attribute IRSs for each risk indicator for each DGS member that is a credit union in each levy cycle:
For a DGS member which is an overseas firm, the PRA will attribute an ARS of 50.

If, at any point, the PRA is unable to calculate the ARS for any DGS member in line with the methodology stated in this chapter (for example, if a DGS member has failed to make a regulatory data submi

The PRA will attribute all DGS members with an ARS, as per the methodology in Chapter 3. The ARS for each firm will be translated into an ARW ranging between 75% – 150%, as specified by the formula in
The adjustment factor µ will be calibrated in each levy cycle to ensure the exact levy target is reached. For example, for a levy target of £80 million:

Were there to be no further adjustment, the total industry levy would exceed the levy target. The adjustment factor µ would then be set here as 80/100(=0.8), and each DGS member’s levy reduced proport
